name: Royal Trail of San Pedro de Rocas
name_original: Camino Real de San Pedro de Rocas – circular por el Monasterio de San Pedro de Rocas y Esgos
description: Hike the Royal Trail of San Pedro de Rocas, a moderate 5.2-mile loop through forests to an ancient rock-carved monastery.
region_intro: Hiking around Esgos offers access to mountainous landscapes and extensive green hills covered in lush forests. The region is characterized by crystal-clear rivers winding through the terrain and is part of the Ribeira Sacra, known for the deep Sil River Canyon. Elevation changes are common, providing varied hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Esgos The most popular hiking route is Camino Real de San Pedro de Rocas – circular by the Monastery of San Pedro de Rocas and Esgos, a 5.2 miles (8.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 29 minutes to complete, passing by the ancient Monastery of San Pedro de Rocas. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ribeira Sacra – circular via the Pé do Home viewpoint and the Sil Canyon, a moderate 10.4 miles (16.8 km) path. This route offers panoramic views of the Sil River Canyon from the Pé do Home viewpoint. Local hikers also love the Route of the mills of the Loña river – circular from A Derrasa, an 8.8 miles (14.2 km) trail leading through river valleys and past historical mills, often completed in about 3 hours 47 minutes. Hiking around Esgos is defined by mountainous landscapes, lush forests, and river canyons, with options ranging from easy strolls to more challenging routes. The network offers options for different ability levels. The routes in Esgos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 170 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Esgos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Esgos?
A: Esgos offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with over 60 trails to explore. These routes cater to different fitness levels, including easy strolls, moderate treks, and a few more challenging options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Esgos?
A: Hiking in Esgos features diverse terrain, from mountainous landscapes and extensive green hills covered in lush forests to crystal-clear rivers winding through the valleys. You'll encounter elevation changes, especially near the dramatic Sil River Canyon, providing varied and engaging experiences.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Esgos?
A: Yes, Esgos has 27 easy hiking routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14935548" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pena do Pobre Viewpoint loop from A Alberguería&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 4 km long and offers pleasant views.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I see while hiking in Esgos?
A: Esgos is rich in both natural beauty and historical sites. Many trails pass by significant landmarks such as the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3136768" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Pedro de Rocas&lt;/a&gt;, partially excavated into rock. You can also find stunning natural viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4213863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedra Longa Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3726234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Boqueiriño Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas of the landscape, including the Sil River Canyon.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Esgos?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Esgos are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975175138/camino-real-de-san-pedro-de-rocas-circular-by-the-monastery-of-san-pedro-de-rocas-and-esgos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Real de San Pedro de Rocas – circular by the Monastery of San Pedro de Rocas and Esgos&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 8.3 km loop.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Esgos?
A: The hiking trails in Esgos are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 170 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the region's stunning natural beauty, the well-maintained paths, and the blend of historical sites with picturesque landscapes.

Q: Are there any hikes that offer views of the Sil River Canyon?
A: Absolutely. Esgos is part of the Ribeira Sacra, which is famous for the dramatic Sil River Canyon. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975193002/ribeira-sacra-circular-via-the-pe-do-home-viewpoint-and-the-sil-canyon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeira Sacra – circular via the Pé do Home viewpoint and the Sil Canyon&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 16.8 km route specifically designed to showcase the breathtaking panoramic views of the canyon from the Pé do Home viewpoint.

Q: Can I find trails that pass by old mills or river valleys?
A: Yes, Esgos offers trails that delve into its historical and natural heritage, including routes along river valleys and past ancient structures. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979344717/route-of-the-mills-of-the-lona-river-circular-from-a-derrasa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the mills of the Loña river – circular from A Derrasa&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 14.2 km trail that leads you through scenic river landscapes and past historical mills.

Q: Are there any other significant religious sites to visit near Esgos besides San Pedro de Rocas?
A: Yes, in addition to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3136768" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Pedro de Rocas&lt;/a&gt;, the region is home to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4012022" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa Cristina&lt;/a&gt;. This significant religious site in the Ribeira Sacra dates back to the 10th century and adds cultural depth to your exploration of the area.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Esgos?
A: Esgos is a great hiking destination throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors, making them ideal. Summer can be warm but is still suitable, especially for early morning or late afternoon hikes. Winter can be cooler but provides a different, serene experience, though some higher elevation trails might be affected by weather.

Q: Are there any trails suitable for families with children?
A: Many of the easy and some moderate trails in Esgos are suitable for families. These routes often feature gentle gradients and interesting points of interest like historical sites or river views, making them engaging for younger hikers. Always check the specific trail details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.
tips: Excavated in the natural rock of the mountains near the town of Esgos in Ourense is the abandoned monastery of San Pedro de Rocas. This ancient cult building is considered one of the oldest in Galicia and the only one that still retains part of its original structure.
Small church on the outskirts of Quinta do Monte. San Bieito has a rectangular plan with a single nave and granite walls.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Royal Trail of San Pedro de Rocas?
faqs.answer: Komoot rates this hike as moderate, primarily due to some gradual ascents and a steeper slope on the return to Esgos. However, many hikers consider it to be of low or fairly easy difficulty, making it suitable for a wide range of abilities, including families.

faqs.question: What are the main attractions along the Royal Trail of San Pedro de Rocas?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the historic Monastery of San Pedro de Rocas, one of the oldest Christian monasteries on the Iberian Peninsula, partly carved into rock. You'll also pass the San Bieito Church, and enjoy sections of the ancient 'royal path' with preserved pavestones, oak and conifer forests, and traditional stone farms.

faqs.question: Is the Royal Trail of San Pedro de Rocas a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Royal Trail of San Pedro de Rocas is a circular route, meaning you'll end up back near your starting point, typically the village of Esgos.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts and ends in the village of Esgos. You should be able to find parking available within or near the village to begin your hike.

faqs.question: Is the Royal Trail of San Pedro de Rocas dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally considered dog-friendly. It's a popular route for those hiking with pets, but always ensure your dog is under control, preferably on a leash, and remember to clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm but is still popular, while winter can be cooler but often provides a serene experience. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the trail or the Ribeira Sacra region?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Royal Trail of San Pedro de Rocas or to access the general Ribeira Sacra region where it's located. The trail and its natural surroundings are freely accessible.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route largely follows the 'Camiño Real de San Pedro de Rocas' and also includes sections of the 'PR-G 4' trail, which is a recognized regional path.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hike?
faqs.answer: You'll walk on an ancient 'royal path' with sections of original side walls and pavestones. The terrain includes paths through serene oak and conifer forests, past various rock formations, and traditional stone-walled farms. There's a gradual ascent towards the monastery and a steeper slope on the return.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restaurants near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends near the village of Esgos. While it's a small village, you may find local establishments like cafes or small restaurants there. It's always a good idea to check in advance for opening hours, especially outside of peak season.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on the Royal Trail of San Pedro de Rocas?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty, comfortable hiking shoes are recommended. Bring water, snacks, sun protection, and appropriate layers for the weather. A camera is also a good idea to capture the historical sites and natural beauty.

waypoints.name: Monastery of San Pedro de Rocas
